headlines.title: Illinois Legislature passes historic AI bill that would require third-party safety audits
headlines.description: The measure, SB315, goes beyond legislation from California and New York regulating America’s largest AI companies. It still needs to be signed by the governor.

headlines.title: Red-hot AI demand pushes more companies into the $1 trillion club
headlines.description: Micron Technologies and SK Hynix joined the trillion-dollar club, which includes Apple, Meta and Tesla.

headlines.title: Gavin Newsom takes a populist turn on AI ahead of a possible 2028 presidential run
headlines.description: AI is becoming a dominant political issue, with many on the right and the left coalescing around concerns about the rapid growth of the industry.

headlines.title: Teachers union president calls for limits on AI and screen time in schools
headlines.description: Randi Weingarten, president of the American Federation of Teachers, is backing a parent-led movement to restrict how much time elementary school students spend on laptops in class.

headlines.title: Tech titans mostly silent after Pope Leo’s warning about risks of AI
headlines.description: Pope Leo XIV’s sweeping warning about the dangers of artificial intelligence drew a largely muted response across the American technology world.

headlines.title: Vance calls Pope Leo’s AI warnings ‘profound’
headlines.description: Vance said in an interview with NBC News he is glad the pope tackled AI. He also discussed why X remains off his phone after he deleted it for Lent.

headlines.title: Pope Leo warns AI is fueling conflict, urges world to slow advances
headlines.description: Pope Leo XIV has called for AI advances to be slowed and blamed the technology for fueling conflict around the world.

headlines.title: 11 presumed dead in Washington state paper mill implosion as rescue shifts to recovery
headlines.description: Washington was bracing for the state’s ‘deadliest industrial tragedy,’ Gov. Bob Ferguson said Wednesday following the rupture of a tank at the Nippon Dynawave plant.

headlines.title: Former CIA officer accused of stashing 300 gold bars in his house
headlines.description: A former CIA officer is accused of stashing more than 300 gold bars in his house, worth $40 million, as well as lying about his educational background. The man claimed he needed the gold bars for “work-related expenses,” according to court documents and two people familiar with his employment history. NBC News' Julia Ainsley breaks down the details in the exclusive story.

headlines.title: Insurance denies coverage for teen’s surgery to treat severe epilepsy
headlines.description: In our series “The Cost of Denial,” NBC News’ Maggie Vespa reports on a 13-year-old Illinois girl who suffers from as many as four seizures a day. Insurance denied coverage for the surgery her doctor said could help, but then reversed its decision days after we asked about the case.

headlines.title: NASA’s Jared Isaacman shares moon base plans
headlines.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman talks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about plans for building a base on the moon, rover designs, and what’s next for the Artemis missions.

headlines.title: Pope Leo issues new warning on artificial intelligence
headlines.description: Pope Leo is making history by wading into the AI debate, warning that people need to be a part of developing the new technology, however it unfolds. NBC News’ Anne Thompson has the story.
